page: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dplsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.4 GHz. The CPU allocation is dedicated, with one thread per core, and includes a cache configuration of 64 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 128 MB L3. The system is equipped with 96.0 GB of memory, providing a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, but is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory performance. The server achieved top-tier results in uncached memory bandwidth for both read and write operations, as well as in the PassMark memory score and database operations. Geekbench multi-core testing also placed in the top tier, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and stress-ng multi-core CPU performance ranked in the strong category. Single-core benchmarks, including stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core, consistently scored in the average range.

The low memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core represents a clear tradeoff, making this server less suitable for memory-intensive applications. This limitation is reflected in LLM inference benchmarks, where prompt processing for large 70B models fell into the weak category. However, the high core count and strong memory bandwidth make it highly effective for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded decompression.
description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Dplsv6 family, featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and physical cores powered by a 3.4 GHz Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server is configured with 96.0 GB of memory, yielding a low memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. In benchmark testing, the instance demonstrates top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation performance, alongside strong multi-core processing capabilities. Conversely, single-core performance and large-model LLM inference remain average to weak. This hardware profile is optimized for compute-bound, multi-threaded tasks such as software compilation, database workloads, and static web serving, where high core density and memory throughput are prioritized over large per-core memory allocations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is a 48-vCPU ARM-based virtual machine featuring a 3.4 GHz Neoverse-N2 processor, 96 GB of RAM, and top-tier memory bandwidth, optimized for multi-threaded compute workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pls_v6 is a 48-vCPU ARM virtual machine with 96 GB RAM, offering top-tier memory bandwidth for multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered virtual machine featuring forty-eight dedicated cores and high memory bandwidth for compute-intensive, multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 48 dedicated ARM Neoverse-N2 physical cores operating at 3.4 GHz
96.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU resources, with Premium Storage support
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
